purpose: Audited SIP conflict suppressions. These pairs are not ignored; they are resolved by explicit precedence, mutually exclusive scope, or strongest-rule-wins aggregation in the AU Horse Analyst resources.

resolved_direction_conflicts.reason: SIP-SL02 applies to front-runners/presser survival on Good tracks. SIP-RW02 applies to S/S- closers facing a stable leader. A single horse cannot be both the protected front-runner and the penalised closer.
Good-track leader EEM support can improve shape, but Good WR failure cap remains a synthesis ceiling unless an explicit override is triggered.
SIP-SL02 protects Good-track front-runners. SIP-RR13 penalises Caulfield Good Rail-True closers. Running-style targets are mutually exclusive.
Randwick leader bonus targets front-runners; RW02 targets S/S- closers behind stable leaders. Apply per horse role.
Randwick leader bonus cannot erase a Good WR cap. The EEM bonus is applied first, then synthesis caps are enforced.
RR10 protects elite trainer raiders/front-runners; RW02 penalises S/S- closers. If a horse is ambiguous, apply RW02 only when its in-run profile is demonstrably closer/settled.
Soft unknown/risk cap is assessed before Soft featherweight bonus. RH02 can lift only horses with sufficient Soft evidence; it cannot override RF02 Tier 4/5 risk by weight alone.
FL01 inner-draw/lightweight bonus is applied to EEM/shape first. FL02 then caps S- if the over-subscribed favourite faces traffic.

resolved_duplicate_counting.reason: All four are EEM geometry/track penalties. They must collapse into one final EEM verdict using the most specific rule, not stack as multiple downgrades.
Apprentice claim, lightweight, inner draw, and Soft featherweight signals share the same physical weight edge. Apply the strongest eligible bonus only.
Elite closer and elite jockey draw overrides both reduce positional penalties. Use the broader valid override once, not twice.
SIP-10 handles general imported-horse tolerance; RH05 is the NZ-specific discount. Use RH05 for NZ raiders, otherwise SIP-10.
All are synthesis caps/floors. Apply all trigger checks, then enforce the most restrictive final cap and log the winning cap only.
Inner congestion, rail-out field scaling, and tight-turn wide-draw penalties are draw geometry effects. Collapse to one draw/EEM verdict by specificity.
Lightweight bonuses are mutually capped by strongest eligible rule.
Soft specialist, Soft place-rate discount, and Soft place-rate rescue form one hierarchy. RR02/RF01 classify first; RR09 applies only if RF01 does not rescue the horse.
Both regulate elite jockey/trainer brand premium. RH03 supersedes RR05 for JMcD/Waller brand-trap conditions.
RR08 is Soft hierarchy; GF02 is Good-track wet-specialist guard. Actual track condition selects the applicable branch.
RR11 is tight-turn sprint wide-draw; RR13 is Caulfield Good closer penalty. If both appear in metadata, the venue/distance-specific rule wins.
